area_anchor: Reception deploy hardening — integrity and config fail-closed
current_truth: Reception still has deploy-facing integrity gaps: account lifecycle partial-failure risk, date-range cleanup holes, non-throwing mutation guards, weak booking-email key semantics, and environment coupling.
next_scope_now: Ship fail-closed behavior for critical mutation/account paths, harden date-range cleanup and booking-email key validation, externalize environment-specific endpoints, and add direct regression tests.
adjacent_later: Introduce server-admin auth management for full auth-user deprovisioning.
Move remaining endpoint constants behind typed runtime config contracts.
evidence_refs: operator-stated: take all non-low reception audit issues forward via lp-do-ideas -> lp-do-build
why: Front-desk operational trust depends on fail-closed writes, consistent cleanup semantics, and deployment-safe configuration boundaries.

intended_outcome.statement: Reception deploy-critical flows fail closed, cleanup invariants hold across multi-day stays, and endpoint/config coupling no longer depends on hardcoded values.
